Name: EarthWorx NW1000 Non-Woven Geotextile - 4.5m x 100m
Description:
EarthWorx NW1000 Non-Woven Geotextile - 4.5m x 100m
&nbsp;

An extra heavy duty, non-woven geotextile with superior durability and mechanical resistance, providing ultimate performance in separation, filtration, drainage and reinforcement making it widely used for roads, highways, car parks, railway construction, foundations and retaining structures.

EarthWorx NW1000 is manufactured from 100% polypropylene fibres to a high specification, which makes it suitable for demanding civil engineering projects particularly those with areas subject to a large volume of vehicular traffic and large imposed loads. This prevents the sub-grade (soil) and sub-base (MOT Type 1 or similar) from mixing, which leads to contamination of the stone sub-base resulting in sinking and rutting of the surface.

Appliations


	Roads
	Car Parks
	Railway Construction
	Foundations
	Retaining Structures
	Horse Arenas
	Highways


Manufacturer: Growtivation

Product Code: EGF450

Name: DrainTex Non-Woven Geotextile Fabric
Description:
DrainTex Geotextile Membrane

Draintex is a standard duty, non-woven drainage geotextile fabric with excellent drainage and filtration properties and is avaiable in 1m x 10m, 2m x 50m, 2m x 25m, 4.5m x 11.1m and 4.5m x 100m.

This drainage geo-textile fabric has a stable but open structure making it ideal for lining trenches when constructing land (French) drains.

Draintex acts as a filter membrane, separating the aggregate from the soil, thus preventing the aggregate from mixing with the soil and reducing the efficiency and capacity of the drain.

DrainTex is also used for wrapping soakaway and water attenuation crates in both small domestic and large commercial projects. The high permeability of the fabric means water can percolate through whilst preventing the soakaway becoming &lsquo;silted&rsquo; up.

DrainTex can be used as a ground stabilisation membrane beneath MOT Type 1 or 3 aggregate or hardcore to form a stable base in many hard landscaping and civil engineering applications.

Appliations


	French/Land Drains
	Wrapping Soakaway Crates
	Driveway Subase

Name: 150mm Heritage Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Eared Slip Coupling
Description:
150mm Heritage Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Eared Slip Coupling
 

Saint-Gobain PAM UK Cast Iron Timesaver Soil Pipe System is Available in a range of pipe diameters with an exstensive range of fittings and to suit any project.

Saint-Gobain PAM UK are market leaders in the supply of Cast iron soil, drainage and rainwater systems.

Benefits of Cast Iron


	Non-combustible fire rated A1
	Excellent acoustic properties
	Minimal expansion
	Strong and robust
	Excellent environmental properties
	Made from up to 99% recycled content
	100% recyclable
	Long life span


Timesaver Soil Coating


	3m Pipes Internally lined with an ochre two-part epoxy coating
	Externally coated in a black acrylic paint.
	Fittings and couplings, Internally and externally coated in a black waterbased paint
	The Timesaver coating shall accept over-coating (Overpainting) with alkyd and water based paints normally used on metallic structures

The Cast Iron Soil Pipe Timesaver range include heritage push-fit couplings that turns a mechanical pipe system into a system with the traditional cast iron appearance.

The Timesaver Heritage couplings have been accepted by recognised bodies for all grade listed properties ie. National Heritage, English Heritage etc. Its primer black coating makes it easy to overpaint for external soil stacks, and is the perfect solution for listed buildings and those situated in areas of conservation.

Prestigious commercial buildings built in the 1970’s to the mid 90’s which are serviced by cast iron soil stacks, will most likely be Timesaver. If the building is to be refurbished and changes are required, the latest Timesaver range is best suited to connect to the original pipework.

Please Note: Timesaver soil fittings were traditionally supplied with a raised bead on the spigot ends, which over the years have been removed. The Heritage couplings can only be used on fittings without the bead.

Manufacturer: Saint-Gobain PAM UK

Product Code: 266538

Name: 100mm Heritage Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Strap On Boss
Description:
100mm Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Strap On Boss
 

Saint-Gobain PAM UK Cast Iron Timesaver Soil Pipe System is Available in a range of pipe diameters with an exstensive range of fittings and to suit any project.

The strap-on boss provides a simple solution for fitting a 50mm copper or waste pipe to an existing 100mm cast iron soil pipe to BS 416 (pipe outside diameter min/max 109/114mm).

Installation


	Simply determine where the waste pipe is to be positioned.
	Cut a 64mm hole into the cast iron soil pipe with a hole saw (the metal from the hole remains in the cutter – see tools below).
	Mechanically fit the boss strap in position (do not forget the rubber washer) tighten until fully secure.
	Insert in the waste pipe until fully seated in the boss.
	Tighten the boss plate to grip the rubber ‘O’ ring on the outside of the waste pipe.


Tools required


	A 64mm hole saw: Product code 192326.
	Arbour: Product code 192327.
	¼" pilot drill: Product code 192328.
	13mm socket EF101: Product code 191202

or


	13mm spanner for mechanically fitting the boss adaptor EF098: Product code 191200.



Saint-Gobain PAM UK are market leaders in the supply of Cast iron soil, drainage and rainwater systems.

	Non-combustible fire rated A1
	Excellent acoustic properties
	Minimal expansion
	Strong and robust

	Made from up to 99% recycled content

Timesaver Soil Coating


	3m Pipes Internally lined with an ochre two-part epoxy coating
	Externally coated in a black acrylic paint.

	The Timesaver coating shall accept over-coating (Overpainting) with alkyd and water based paints normally used on metallic structures

The Cast Iron Soil Pipe Timesaver range include heritage push-fit couplings that turns a mechanical pipe system into a system with the traditional cast iron appearance.

The Timesaver Heritage couplings have been accepted by recognised bodies for all grade listed properties ie. National Heritage, English Heritage etc. Its primer black coating makes it easy to overpaint for external soil stacks, and is the perfect solution for listed buildings and those situated in areas of conservation.

Prestigious commercial buildings built in the 1970’s to the mid 90’s which are serviced by cast iron soil stacks, will most likely be Timesaver. If the building is to be refurbished and changes are required, the latest Timesaver range is best suited to connect to the original pipework.

Please Note: Timesaver soil fittings were traditionally supplied with a raised bead on the spigot ends, which over the years have been removed. The Heritage couplings can only be used on fittings without the bead.

Prestigious commercial buildings built in the 1970&rsquo;s to the mid 90&rsquo;s which are serviced by cast iron soil stacks, will most likely be Timesaver. If the building is to be refurbished and changes are required, the latest Timesaver range is best suited to connect to the original pipework.

Please Note: Timesaver soil fittings were traditionally supplied with a raised bead on the spigot ends, which over the years have been removed. The couplings can only be used on fittings without the bead.

Name: 100mm Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Strap On Boss
Description:
100mm Timesaver Cast Iron Soil Pipe Strap On Boss
&nbsp;

Saint-Gobain PAM UK Cast Iron Timesaver Soil Pipe System is Available in a range of pipe diameters with an exstensive range of fittings and to suit any project.

The strap-on boss provides a simple solution for fitting a 50mm copper or waste pipe to an existing 100mm cast iron soil pipe to BS 416 (pipe outside diameter min/max 109/114mm).

Installation


	Simply determine where the waste pipe is to be positioned.
	Cut a 64mm hole into the cast iron soil pipe with a hole saw (the metal from the hole remains in the cutter &ndash; see tools below).
	Mechanically fit the boss strap in position (do not forget the rubber washer) tighten until fully secure.
	Insert in the waste pipe until fully seated in the boss.
	Tighten the boss plate to grip the rubber &lsquo;O&rsquo; ring on the outside of the waste pipe.


Tools required


	A 64mm hole saw: Product code 192326.
	Arbour: Product code 192327.
	&frac14;&quot; pilot drill: Product code 192328.
	13mm socket EF101: Product code 191202

or


	13mm spanner for mechanically fitting the boss adaptor EF098: Product code 191200.

	Externally coated in a black acrylic paint.
	Fittings and couplings, Internally and externally coated in a black waterbased paint
	The Timesaver coating shall accept over-coating (Overpainting) with alkyd and water based paints normally used on metallic structures

The Cast Iron Soil Pipe Timesaver range include heritage push-fit couplings that turns a mechanical pipe system into a system with the traditional cast iron appearance.

The Timesaver heritage couplings have been accepted by recognised bodies for all grade listed properties ie. National Heritage, English etc. Its primer black coating makes it easy to overpaint for external soil stacks, and is the perfect solution for listed buildings and those situated in areas of conservation.

Prestigious commercial buildings built in the 1970&rsquo;s to the mid 90&rsquo;s which are serviced by cast iron soil stacks, will most likely be Timesaver. If the building is to be refurbished and changes are required, the latest Timesaver range is best suited to connect to the original pipework.

Please Note: Timesaver soil fittings were traditionally supplied with a raised bead on the spigot ends, which over the years have been removed. The couplings can only be used on fittings without the bead.

Manufacturer: Saint-Gobain PAM UK

Name: Lindab Magestic Galvanised Steel Rectangular 70mm Flex-Fit Fascia Bracket 140mm
Description:
Lindab Magestic Galvanised Steel Rectangular Gutter 70mm Flex-Fit Fascia Bracket RTK07140 140mm


	
	Uses flexible clips to hold the gutter in place
	
	
	Recommended 600 mm spacing, should be installed to create fall of 10 mm per 3m run
	
	
	70 mm bracket with 2 fixing holes
	



The Lindab Magestic Range of Rectangular gutters is an enhanced galvanised steel material for rainwater systems and standing seam applications.

Lindab Magestic Steel offers the same trusted Lindab product designs with a strengthened magnesium zinc alloy coating.

Drainage Central offer the full Lindab Magestic Galvanised Steel range, which come in various gutter lengths along with fittings to suit including fascia brackets, rafter brackets, gutter joints and gutter outlets.

Lindab Magestic Steel Guttering and downpipe come with a range of benefits which you can see below.

Elite Galvanisation
The steel from Lindab is developed using magnesium to create a barrier to protect zincs porous structure against corrosion factors.

Proven Protection
Lindab have carried out Salt spray tests to illustrate the ultimate surface protection of any metallic coated steel. Magestic Galvanised Steel has more than twenty times the corrosion resistance of hot dipped galvanised steel.

Reduced Coastal Corrosion
Magestic corrosion is not influenced by the chlorine content of the environment. Natural exposure tests show more than three times the corrosion resistance of standard galvanisation in marine environments.

Self-Healing 
Lindab Galvanised coatings transfer to repair red rust on cuts and scratches. Magnesium is more anodic than zinc making the sacrificial properties of Magestic more active than standard galvanisation.

Lower Environmental Impact 
Lindab Magestic Galvanised steel is the most environmentally friendly rainwater systems on the market. Lindab Magestic Galvanised Steel has a decreased effect on zinc resources and less than 25% zinc run off into soils compared to hot dipped galvanised steel.

Ages Beautifully 
Lindab Steel Unpainted metal gutters weather naturally organically adjusting to the environment. Magestic Galvanised steel settles to an attractive dark grey metallic appearance within 3-5 years after installation.

10 year warranty 
Lindab Magestic products all come with a 10 year warranty.

Manufacturer: Lindab

Product Code: RTK07140MG

Drainage Central offer the full Lindab Magestic Galvanised Steel range, which come in various gutter lengths along with fittings to suit including fascia brackets, rafter brackets, gutter joints and gutter outlets.

Lindab Magestic Steel Guttering and downpipe come with a range of benefits which you can see below.

Elite Galvanisation
The steel from Lindab is developed using magnesium to create a barrier to protect zincs porous structure against corrosion factors.

Proven Protection
Lindab have carried out Salt spray tests to illustrate the ultimate surface protection of any metallic coated steel. Magestic Galvanised Steel has more than twenty times the corrosion resistance of hot dipped galvanised steel.

Reduced Coastal Corrosion
Magestic corrosion is not influenced by the chlorine content of the environment. Natural exposure tests show more than three times the corrosion resistance of standard galvanisation in marine environments.

Self-Healing 
Lindab Galvanised coatings transfer to repair red rust on cuts and scratches. Magnesium is more anodic than zinc making the sacrificial properties of Magestic more active than standard galvanisation.

Lower Environmental Impact 
Lindab Magestic Galvanised steel is the most environmentally friendly rainwater systems on the market. Lindab Magestic Galvanised Steel has a decreased effect on zinc resources and less than 25% zinc run off into soils compared to hot dipped galvanised steel.

Ages Beautifully 
Lindab Steel Unpainted metal gutters weather naturally organically adjusting to the environment. Magestic Galvanised steel settles to an attractive dark grey metallic appearance within 3-5 years after installation.

10 year warranty 
Lindab Magestic products all come with a 10 year warranty.

Manufacturer: Lindab

Product Code: SKK100MG

Natural Copper Downpipe from Lindab is an exceptional rainwater system, due to its remarkable aesthetics and range of benefits you can only get with copper gutters and downpipes.

Copper provides a range of qualities that contributes to providing an excellent rainwater system, the material is exceptionally durable due to its natural corrosion resistance and is expected to last up to a century.

Copper is also naturally poisonous to plant life, meaning less maintenance to remove any moss or algae growth.

The Natural Copper range of downpipes is one of the most environmentally friendly rainwater systems available on the market. This is due to the materials being manufactured using low carbon emissions processes and the natural copper can be completely recycled at the end of its life span.

Due to a chemical reaction known as oxidation, Copper naturally becomes green throughout the years as it reacts with oxygen in the air. The green colour is the natural shade of the patina that forms over the copper surface which protects the copper from deterioration and creates a light bluish-green facade.

The green colour is the natural shade of the patina that forms over the copper surface which protects the copper from deterioration and creates a light bluish-green facade.

Why Choose Lindab Steel Guttering For your project?

When it comes to choosing a new rainwater system to install, there is a wide range of systems to choose form each with their own advantages and disadvantages. Steel guttering and downpipes come with many desirable benefits, not only by aesthetically improving your project.

The Benefits of using Lindab Steel rainwater systems is because they are Lightweight, Durable, Cost effective and more environmentally friendly than any other material on the market.

Steel Gutter and downpipes are much lighter and durable than other rainwater systems on the market, providing minimal movement and UV stability, will not crack, fade, or leak. It is highly resistant to corrosion, virtually maintenance free and vandal-proof.

Lindab Rainline Steel Gutter and Downpipes offer some of the highest quality on the market when it comes to combining durability, flexibility, and ease of installation.

The Lindab Rainline system has been developed by professionals over time and has grown to become a top choice for many tradesmen and DIY enthusiasts.

Lindab painted steel gutter and downpipe is rustproofed by a 275g zinc coating. The galvanised coating is naturally self-healing, any scratch or cut is automatically sealed by zinc ions which migrate to re-coat the uncovered steel.

Lindab Rainline is manufactured from up to 30% recycled material and is itself completely recyclable. The system will last more than 70 years, reducing the impact on the environment. The Lindab Majestic Galvanised and Rain line systems are simple to fit and will last for decades.

Drainage Central also supplies the Lindab Magestic Galvanised Steel Range of gutters and downpipes that are also covered by 10-year warranty (See more details in the Lindab Colour Options Tab Above).

Why Choose Lindab Steel Guttering For your project?

When it comes to choosing a new rainwater system to install, there is a wide range of systems to choose form each with their own advantages and disadvantages. Steel guttering and downpipes come with many desirable benefits, not only by aesthetically improving your project.

The Benefits of using Lindab Steel rainwater systems is because they are Lightweight, Durable, Cost effective and more environmentally friendly than any other material on the market.

Steel Gutter and downpipes are much lighter and durable than other rainwater systems on the market, providing minimal movement and UV stability, will not crack, fade, or leak. It is highly resistant to corrosion, virtually maintenance free and vandal-proof.

Lindab Rainline Steel Gutter and Downpipes offer some of the highest quality on the market when it comes to combining durability, flexibility, and ease of installation.

The Lindab Rainline system has been developed by professionals over time and has grown to become a top choice for many tradesmen and DIY enthusiasts.

Lindab painted steel gutter and downpipe is rustproofed by a 275g zinc coating. The galvanised coating is naturally self-healing, any scratch or cut is automatically sealed by zinc ions which migrate to re-coat the uncovered steel.

Lindab Rainline is manufactured from up to 30% recycled material and is itself completely recyclable. The system will last more than 70 years, reducing the impact on the environment. The Lindab Majestic Galvanised and Rain line systems are simple to fit and will last for decades.

Manufacturer: Lindab

Name: Dry Verge Angled Ridge Cap
Description:
Dry Verge Angled Ridge Cap

Suitable on new build roofs or roofs where the extisting verge is being renewed. The Elite Dry Verge is designed to provide a simple, secure method of mechanically fixing verge tiles. Reduces the risk of wind damage and water penetration


	100% Weather Proof
	Maintenance Free
	Speeds on-site installation
	Ultimate protection


Unique New Features


	Front concealed fixing - a unique extra leg allows a front face fixing. Verge units slide or click together from top to bottom, leaving the tile undisturbed.
	Designed for maximum tile overhang - fits straight onto the roof for immediate connection.
	No unnecessary cutting or moving tiles - no reason to disturb tiles due to the 65mm overhang. Saves time and costs by reducing risk damage to the roof.
	Allows all weather fitting - face fixings allows all weather fixing and less installation time.
	Manufactured from UV stabilised and recyclable polypropylene
	Grey and Brown colour options which blend with the roof
	Compatibility with a large range of interlocking concrete tiles between 280mm - 345mm gauge
	Quick and easy to fix, no special tools required
	Allows all weather fixing
	Saves on time and labour
	Provides that added finish when used with PVC fascias and soffits


Manufacturer: Ariel Plastics
Product Code: RCG-A (Grey) - RCB-A (Brown)

	easy installation by simply pushing the two metre lengths between the gutter and the roof tiles
	fits inside the gutter creating a screen from debris entering the system
	maintenance free
	flexible central rust-free stainless steel core
	helps prevent entry of birds into the roof space
	can be installed into channel drain
	white option excellent for low level white gutters, such as conservatories
